title: Edithea guerrerensis
intro: Edithea guerrerensis, also known as Mexican Damiana, is a flowering plant native to the arid regions of Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and reproductive issues due to its adaptogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Edithea guerrerensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Mexican Damiana include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and enhance overall well-being.
